title: Calycogonium moanum
intro: Calycogonium moanum, also known as Hawaiian coral bell, is a type of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calycogonium moanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Calycogonium moanum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make salves, ointments, and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Calycogonium moanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Miconiaceae. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the order Oxalidales. This plant is native to Hawaii and is also known to be found in other parts of the Pacific Islands. The classification of Calycogonium moanum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids. Calycogonium moanum is often found growing in a variety of environments, including forests and rocky areas.
uses: Calycogonium moanum, also known as the Hawaiian coral tree or ʻohe hano ihu, has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and roots are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Additionally, it is believed to have analgesic and antipyretic properties, allowing it to help alleviate pain and reduce fever. The plant is also used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Calycogonium moanum.

side_effects: Calycogonium moanum, also known as red ma'o or Hawaiian basketflower, is a plant native to Hawaii that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
